title: Faulty Rotated Binary Search with Duplicate Minima

statement.markdown: Implement a function to perform binary search on a rotated sorted array `nums` with duplicate minima. If the array is not rotated, the array is sorted in ascending order. If the array is rotated, the minimum element will be at a position greater than the initial index 0. For example, if the array is [5, 7, 7, 8, 9, 1, 3], the rotated array is sorted first after the first element 5. The function should return the index of the target number `target` if it exists in the array. Otherwise, return -1.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes two parameters: a rotated sorted array `nums` and a target number `target`.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the index of the target number if it exists. Otherwise, return -1.

examples.explanation: The target number 1 exists in the array at index 5.
The target number 1 exists in the array at index 2.
The target number 1 exists in the array at index 1.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: We can use binary search to find the index of the target number. However, since the array has duplicate minima, we need to handle this case separately. We can use a helper function to find the minimum element in the array.
editorial.bruteForce: We can sort the array first and then use binary search to find the target number. However, this approach has a time complexity of O(n log n) and is not efficient.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a binary search approach with a helper function to find the minimum element in the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a binary search approach with a helper function to find the minimum element in the array. We can compare the target number with the minimum element and adjust the search range accordingly.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the mathematical property of the binary search algorithm.
editorial.dryRun: We can perform a dry run to test the solution with sample inputs.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the case of duplicate minima.
Using a brute force approach instead of binary search.
